cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what upset Melanie during the National Championships. The options are A to D. Let me read through the passage again to find the relevant part. In the third paragraph, Melanie talks about entering the National Championships. She mentions that she had the second fastest swim and the fastest run but "came nowhere." Then she says, "I was following this man and suddenly we came to the sea. We realised then that we had gone wrong. I ended up cycling 20 kilometres too far. I cried all the way through the running." So the key point here is that she made a mistake in the race, specifically during the cycling part. She followed someone and took a wrong turn, which led her to cycle an extra 20 km. That must have cost her a lot of energy and time, hence her crying during the run. Looking at the options: (C) She made a mistake during part of the race. That seems to fit. Option A says she was tricked by another competitor, but the text says she was following a man, not necessarily tricked. It might have been a mistake on her part.
